themeWords.meaning: Saul; 'asked for'; first king of Israel
Philistines; a people of Philistia, Israel's coastal foes

sentenceTheme: Saul and his sons fall in battle against the Philistines; his body is dishonored, brave men of Jabesh recover the remains, and Saul dies because of his unfaithfulness, so the kingdom is turned to David by the LORD.

verses.text: Now the Philistines fought against Israel; and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and fell down slain in mount Gilboa.

highlightedWords.meaning: Philistines; a people of Philistia, Israel's coastal foes
Israel; the people/nation descended from Jacob

verses.text: And the Philistines followed hard after Saul, and after his sons; and the Philistines slew Jonathan, and Abinadab, and Malchishua, the sons of Saul.

highlightedWords.meaning: Philistines; a people of Philistia, Israel's coastal foes
Saul; 'asked for'; first king of Israel

verses.text: And the battle went sore against Saul, and the archers hit him, and he was wounded of the archers.

highlightedWords.meaning: battle, war, warfare
Saul; 'asked for'; first king of Israel

verses.text: Then said Saul to his armourbearer, Draw thy sword, and thrust me through therewith; lest these uncircumcised come and abuse me. But his armourbearer would not; for he was sore afraid. So Saul took a sword, and fell upon it.

highlightedWords.meaning: sword; dagger; destructive instrument of war
to fear; be afraid; stand in awe

verses.text: And when his armourbearer saw that Saul was dead, he fell likewise on the sword, and died.

highlightedWords.meaning: sword; dagger; destructive instrument of war
to die; to perish; to be put to death

verses.text: So Saul died, and his three sons, and all his house died together.

highlightedWords.meaning: to die; to perish; to be put to death
house; household; family; dwelling

verses.text: And when all the men of Israel that were in the valley saw that they fled, and that Saul and his sons were dead, then they forsook their cities, and fled: and the Philistines came and dwelt in them.

highlightedWords.meaning: valley; broad, deep plain
city; town; urban center

verses.text: And it came to pass on the morrow, when the Philistines came to strip the slain, that they found Saul and his sons fallen in mount Gilboa.

highlightedWords.meaning: Philistines; a people of Philistia, Israel's coastal foes
slain; pierced; one fatally wounded

verses.text: And when they had stripped him, they took his head, and his armour, and sent into the land of the Philistines round about, to carry tidings unto their idols, and to the people.

highlightedWords.meaning: head; chief; top; summit
vessel; implement; weapon/armour

verses.text: And they put his armour in the house of their gods, and fastened his head in the temple of Dagon.

highlightedWords.meaning: God; gods; divine beings
Dagon; a Philistine deity

verses.text: And when all Jabesh-gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul,

highlightedWords.meaning: Philistines; a people of Philistia, Israel's coastal foes
Saul; 'asked for'; first king of Israel

verses.text: They arose, all the valiant men, and took away the body of Saul, and the bodies of his sons, and brought them to Jabesh, and buried their bones under the oak in Jabesh, and fasted seven days.

highlightedWords.meaning: strength, might; valour; able/valiant
bone; bones; substance

verses.text: So Saul died for his transgression which he committed against the LORD, even against the word of the LORD, which he kept not, and also for asking counsel of one that had a familiar spirit, to enquire of it;

highlightedWords.meaning: the LORD; the personal name of Israel's God
word; matter; decree

verses.text: And enquired not of the LORD: therefore he slew him, and turned the kingdom unto David the son of Jesse.

highlightedWords.meaning: kingdom; dominion; reign
David; beloved; second king of Israel
